title: Pre recorded Lecture (Design Thinking Double Diamond Approach)
body:
Social Entrepreneurship Practicum

L ecture : Design Thinking & the Double Diamond

Dr Linh Nguyen, Course Convenor Lecturer UNSW Centre for Social Impact Email: linh-nguyen@unsw.edu.au   Acknowledgement of Country

We acknowledge the Traditional Custodians of the lands where we currently live, work and play. We pay our deep respects and give thanks to Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Elders past and present for their custodianship of Country.  Agenda What is Design & Design Thinking?

Why does Design Thinking matter?

The Double Diamond – a design model

Design for Social Change

Design and Design T hinking  Design is …  Design is about what things look like

Photo: housebeautiful.com Photo: Auto & Design

Design is about aesthetics

Photo: Designstak.co Photo: Frepik.com

Design is about functionality/ usage

Photo: Etsy Photo: Amazon

Design is not just it looks like and feels like. Design is how it works

Steve Jobs

Design is what happens when people use creativity to solve problems. Everything not made by nature , has been designed.

Design Council https://www.designcouncil.org.uk/our -work/what -is -design/

"Design isn’t just about making things;

It’s about making lives, communities and

industries better."

(Design Council)  What is Design Thinking & Why is it important?  Design Thinking is …

a mindset and approach to problem-solving and innovation anchored around human-centered design (Han 2022)

an effective model for identifying and understanding the relationship between problems and solution. The processes enable a more humancentred outcome that is more about the consumer experience than a consumer product (Brown and Wyatt 2010)

a human-centered innovation process that emphasizes observation, collaboration, fast learning, visualization of ideas, rapid concept prototyping, and concurrent business analysis, which ultimately influences innovation and business strategy (PWC)

Why Design Thinking Skills Matter? As of December 2021, the most common occupations requiring design thinking skills were: ▪ Marketing managers ▪ Executives ▪ Industrial engineers ▪ Graphic designers ▪ Software developers ▪ General and operations managers ▪ Management analysts ▪ Personal service managers ▪ Architectural and engineering managers ▪ Computer and information systems managers (Han 2022)

D esign T hinking S kills & Growing Demand

▪ Jobs that require design thinking statistically have higher salaries (Emsi Burning Glass 2021)

▪ Businesses are looking for talent with design thinking skills (Han 2022)

▪ Research found that businesses that used design thinking approach were doing economically better (Design Management Institute)

The Explainer: What Is Design Thinking? Havard Business Review

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=_WI3B54m6SU  The Design Thinking Process: The double diamond approach

The Design Thinking Process: The double diamond approach

Finding the right Finding the right problem solution

UK Design Council  ‘To get a good idea, you need a lot of ideas.’ Linus Pauling, the only person in history to win two individual Nobel Prizes  Human-centred innovation –understanding your users/costumers  What is Human Centered Design?

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=musmgKEPY2o 21   Human-centred innovation: Evaluate ideas/solutions to determine which to focus on to deliver the greatest impact

Viability The Sweet Can the opportunity be delivered within available budgets, and deliver a powerful ROI (eg reducing Spot for admin costs, wait times).

Innovation

Desirability Feasibility What is the human impact of the opportunity? E.g Technical capabilities in the field of digital and other Will it move the needle on delivering a superior emerging technologies, we need to assess patient experience and empower the hospital application potential and ability to link with existing teams IT systems and programs.

Source: Professor Martin Tomitsch, University of Technology Sydney  Prototyping  What is a Prototype?

A prototype is an early, rough version of a product or concept that is developed to test and validate ideas, gather user feedback, and identify potential issues before finalizing the design (IDO)   Different types of Prototyping Sketches & drawings Experiences Physical models

Image: Sunny Cui - Medium Students in the d.school class “Design Thinking Bootcamp” https://news.mit.edu/2020/integrating-electronics-physicalprototyping the patient experience. Fall 2011. prototypes-0304 https://publicpolicyinnovators.wordpress.com/portfolio/ex perience-prototyping/

Prototyping: Other Resources Rapid Prototyping: Sketching | Google for Startups (youtube.com) Rapid Prototyping: Digital | Google for Startups (youtube.com) Rapid Prototyping: Native | Google for Startups (youtube.com) Make Your Ideas Tangible Through Prototypes – IDEO U

Design Thinking & Social Enterprises Human-Centered Focus: Solutions deeply rooted in empathy ensure relevance and impactful outcomes for community needs.

Collaboration and Co-creation: Engaging diverse stakeholders fosters holistic solutions reflecting multiple perspectives.

Innovation: Encourages creative approaches to solve entrenched social problems and find novel solutions.

Iterative Problem-Solving: Continuous testing and feedback cycles improve solution effectiveness and adaptability.

Sustainability and Scalability: Creates environmentally friendly, economically viable solutions capable of broad replication.

Design for Social • Human-Centered Focus, Empathy: Deep understanding of poor villagers’ financial Impact struggles was gained by immersive observation and empathy. • Problem of Collateral: Lack of collateral excluded the poor from traditional banking, exposing them to exploitative lenders. • Ideation and innovative solution: Group lending used social collateral, fostering trust and accountability among small borrower groups. • Prototyping and testing: The model was initially tested on a small scale in the village of Jobra before being refined and expanded. • Impact, Sustainability, Scalability: The model was designed to be replicable, allowing it to scale across Bangladesh and later internationally  Design Thinking - IDEO
